message:
[high] jira: pin base_url/agile_url/automation_url origins per deployment

from_params accepted three URL overrides (base_url, agile_url,
automation_url) straight from the configure payload with zero
validation — no scheme check, no host allowlist, no empty/blank
rejection. A configure payload could redirect every Jira REST,
Agile, and Automation call to an attacker-controlled host before
any request was issued.

- New validate_jira_endpoint_url(raw, field, deployment, domain)
  enforces deployment-aware trust rules:
  * JiraDeployment::Cloud pins the host to `{domain}.atlassian.net`
    so acme2.atlassian.net can't be smuggled past an acme tenant.
  * JiraDeployment::ServerDc accepts any https host (self-hosted
    installations legitimately live anywhere) but still rejects
    plain http on a non-local host.
  * Localhost/127.0.0.1/::1 accepted under both deployments for
    test harnesses; wiremock-driven tests continue to work.
  * URL parsing rejects substring smuggles like
    https://evil.com/acme.atlassian.net.
- from_params now trims and discards empty overrides before
  validation so " " doesn't silently propagate.
- Seven new unit tests cover Cloud host pin, cross-tenant reject,
  substring smuggle reject, localhost accept, ServerDc accept,
  ServerDc http reject, and empty/malformed reject.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
